_comment: Evaluation scenarios that must NOT appear in the training corpus. Global rule of the brief: 'exclude any text overlapping our evaluation scenario'. This file is CONFIGURATION: it lives with the code (src/common/), it is read by common/contamination.py, and it applies to cat1, cat2 and cat3 without any code change. Adding an evaluation scenario = adding an entry here.

_rule_types.phrase: contaminating if any of the phrases appears verbatim (case-insensitive, whitespace normalised, accents stripped).
_rule_types.cooccurrence: contaminating if AT LEAST one term from EACH group appears within window_chars characters of a term from every other group. This avoids false positives such as 'fire' alone in a safety manual, or 'metro' alone in a postal address.

_note_on_terms: The term lists deliberately mix English and French spellings. Matching is done on accent-stripped text, so 'metro' also catches 'metro' written with an accent, and OCR output that lost its accents is still caught.

scenarios.description: Project evaluation scenario: a fire in a subway station, handled by a team of heterogeneous robots. Any text overlapping this scenario must stay out of the DAPT corpus.

phrases: incendie dans une station de metro
